ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general sesame paste entry (FOODON_03311737); no specific black sesame paste entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated black sesame paste code. A00LC (sesame seeds and sesame products) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for black sesame paste as a prepared food item.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 g (one bowl), which is the typical portion size served at Chinese dessert stalls in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Sesame paste (black sesame hu) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: Sesame seeds, whole, roasted and toasted (NDB 12036) and Peanuts, all types, dry-roasted (NDB 16090), combined with USDA Foundation Foods black rice and red dates data; values weighted by approximate ingredient proportions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for traditional handmade black sesame paste as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for the primary ingredients: black sesame seeds (~55% by dry weight), black rice (~15%), peanuts (~10%), red dates (~10%), sugar (~8%), water (~2%). Energy and macros are weighted composites. This is a medium-confidence estimate. HPB Singapore Foodo does not carry a direct entry for this specific composite dessert.

gi_source.title: Sesame paste / black sesame dessert — analogous estimate based on Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. Black sesame seeds and sesame-based pastes are analogous to other seed-based pastes with low GI (~35) due to high fat, fibre, and protein content moderating glucose response.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for traditional black sesame paste in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 35 is estimated by analogy with sesame-based seed pastes and tahini (GI ~40, Atkinson et al. 2008) and adjusted downward slightly for the high fat and fibre content of black sesame. The added sugar component (approximately 8% by weight) moderates the overall GI upward slightly from pure sesame. GL per 250 g serving = (35 × 38.0 × 2.5) / 100 = 33.25, rounded to 33.3.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content (~18 mg/100 g); rich in magnesium and potassium which support vascular tone
hypertension.guidance: Black sesame paste is naturally low in sodium and provides magnesium (~195 mg/100 g) and potassium (~420 mg/100 g), both of which support healthy blood pressure; individuals with hypertension may consume moderate portions but should monitor any added sugar or salt in commercial preparations.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load per serving due to added sugar and black rice; high fat and fibre content attenuates glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although black sesame paste has a low estimated GI (~35) owing to its high fat, protein, and fibre content, a standard 250 g bowl delivers approximately 33 g of glycemic load; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to 100–150 g and choose preparations with reduced added sugar to manage postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High unsaturated fat content (MUFA + PUFA ~17.7 g/100 g) from sesame and peanuts; zero cholesterol; rich in lignans and sesamin with antioxidant properties
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Black sesame paste provides predominantly unsaturated fats and is cholesterol-free, with sesame lignans (sesamin, sesamolin) that have demonstrated antioxidant and LDL-lowering properties in peer-reviewed studies; moderate regular consumption as part of a balanced diet is generally considered cardioprotective, though total caloric density (~385 kcal/100 g) warrants portion control.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Black sesame paste (黑芝麻糊, Hēi Zhīma Hú) is a traditional Chinese sweet dessert soup or porridge made primarily from roasted and finely ground black sesame seeds, typically enriched with complementary ingredients such as black rice, red dates (jujube), peanuts, and sugar. The dish originates from southern Chinese culinary tradition and is widely consumed across China, Taiwan, Hong Kong, and in Chinese diaspora communities throughout Southeast Asia, including Singapore where it is a staple of Chinese dessert stalls (糖水铺). The preparation involves dry-roasting black sesame seeds to develop a deep, nutty aroma, then grinding them into a fine powder before cooking with water and other ingredients into a smooth, velvety paste or thick soup. The texture is silky and creamy, the colour is deep black, and the flavour is intensely nutty, slightly sweet, and earthy. Nutritionally, black sesame paste is calorie-dense (~385 kcal/100 g) and rich in healthy unsaturated fats, plant protein (~11.5 g/100 g), dietary fibre (~6.5 g/100 g), calcium (~680 mg/100 g), iron (~7.8 mg/100 g), magnesium, and zinc. It is a notable plant-based source of calcium and iron. The high fat and fibre content results in a low estimated glycemic index (~35), making it a relatively moderate glycemic food despite the added sugar. In Traditional Chinese Medicine, black sesame is associated with nourishing the liver and kidneys and is considered beneficial for hair health and anti-ageing. Clinically, the dish is low in sodium and cholesterol-free, making it suitable for most adults in moderate portions; however, its caloric density and moderate glycemic load per serving (~33 GL units per 250 g bowl) require portion awareness for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or obesity. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free, though peanut and sesame allergens are present.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA) covers this specific composite traditional black sesame paste dish with all four enriching ingredients. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for the primary ingredients weighted by approximate proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on sesame-based paste analogues from the University of Sydney GI Database literature. Medium confidence is appropriate.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are ingredient-weighted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for black sesame seeds (NDB 12036), peanuts dry-roasted (NDB 16090), black rice, and red dates. No direct composite dish entry exists in any approved primary source for this specific formulation. 2. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero as black sesame seeds, peanuts, black rice, and red dates contain no industrially produced trans fats; naturally zero by ingredient composition. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as all ingredients are plant-based; naturally zero. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as this is an entirely plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients. 5. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 — naturally zero as plant-based sesame paste contains no significant vitamin D; not reported in USDA SR Legacy for sesame seeds. 6. monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated at 8.2 g from USDA sesame seed fatty acid profile). 7. polyunsaturated_fat_g estimated at 9.5 g from USDA sesame seed fatty acid profile. 8. GI of 35 is an analogous estimate; no direct measurement exists in the University of Sydney GI Database for this composite dish. Basis: tahini/sesame paste analogues (GI ~40, Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care) adjusted for higher fibre and fat content of this formulation. 9. The input description mentions 'Xinjiang red dates' and 'Shandong black sesame seeds' as premium regional ingredients; these are preserved in the ingredient context but do not alter the nutrient profile materially. 10. Serving size of 250 g is based on standard Chinese dessert stall bowl portion. GL = (35 × 38.0 × 2.5) / 100 = 33.25 ≈ 33.3. 11. FoodOn URI FOODON_03311737 is used as closest parent; no exact FoodOn entry for black sesame paste exists as of April 2026. 12. FoodEx2 code A00LC is the closest parent category for sesame products; no dedicated black sesame paste code exists in FoodEx2 as of April 2026.
